outcome.what_verifier_checked: Case-insensitive string equality between /app/answer.txt and the expected answer 'Rd5'.
outcome.headline: Agent misread the chess image into a FEN where no forced win exists for Black, then looped on Stockfish queries until the 600s agent timeout — never writing /app/answer.txt.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt was ever written; Stockfish on the agent's FEN repeatedly returned 'bestmove d4f5' with cp ~13 (drawn).

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent re-analysis until timeout
failure_modes.description: After the first Stockfish run failed to show a winning move, the agent kept re-running essentially the same analysis (higher depth, mate-search, multipv, board rotation, Lichess cloud eval) instead of committing any answer to /app/answer.txt, exhausting the 600s budget and triggering AgentTimeoutError.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent board reinterpretation
failure_modes.description: After the engine result did not fit its expectation, the agent repeatedly changed or questioned the board orientation and piece identities, including testing a rotated FEN, consuming the remaining budget without converging on a submitted move.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Correcting FEN Interpretation: I've been working to get the correct interpretation of the FEN string. I initially thought the board might be rotated, leading to misinterpretations of the files and ranks. Now, I've confirmed that the standard orientation is assumed with 'a1' at the bottom-left. Following this, I've re-read the FEN and found the mistake: the White King is on a8, not h1.

failure_modes.name: Correct engine candidate not converted to answer
failure_modes.description: Stockfish output exposed the canonical move as d8d5, which corresponds to the expected algebraic answer Rd5, but the agent treated the low centipawn score as a contradiction and kept searching instead of writing the answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: info depth 7 seldepth 6 multipv 1 score cp 367 nodes 781 nps 260333 hashfull 0 tbhits 0 time 3 pv d8d5

counterfactual.X: At step 8 or 9, convert Stockfish's d8d5 candidate to algebraic notation and write `Rd5` to /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The exact-answer verifier would have matched the expected answer Rd5.

failure_modes.name: Long-running malformed Stockfish probe consumed budget
failure_modes.description: The agent launched a MultiPV-style Stockfish check that blocked until the harness cancelled it, wasting most of the remaining task time and contributing directly to the final timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

failure_modes.name: Misencoded board orientation
failure_modes.description: The agent mapped the labeled image (h-file on the left, row 1 at top) directly into a standard FEN without reconciling orientation, then treated Stockfish’s small positive scores as proof the puzzle had no forced win. Early engine output included pv d8d5 at depth 7, but the agent fixated on bestmove d4f5 from deeper searches instead of converting d8d5 to algebraic Rd5.

failure_modes.name: Image-to-FEN misperception
failure_modes.description: Agent parsed the puzzle image into a FEN that does not match the actual position; Stockfish therefore evaluated the position as roughly equal (cp ~13) and never returned the puzzle answer Rd5 (uci d8d5). The whole subsequent analysis was rooted in this wrong board state.

counterfactual.X: After Stockfish reported only a +0.1 cp evaluation on a position the prompt guarantees is winning for Black, treat that as a contradiction and re-derive piece placement from the image (or use OCR / python-chess to double-check) before continuing.
counterfactual.Y: A corrected FEN would surface Rd5 (d8d5) as the winning move, which the agent could then write to /app/answer.txt.

failure_modes.name: Vision-driven FEN misidentification
failure_modes.description: The agent's reconstructed FEN never yielded a winning evaluation, indicating at least one piece was misread from the image; the agent flip-flopped on board orientation ('h at the bottom-left' vs 'a8 at the bottom-right') and on whether the b3 piece was a queen/rook/bishop, but never reconciled the contradiction with the task's stated guarantee.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: could the Queen on b3 actually be a Black Bishop?

failure_modes.name: Wrong board state decoded from the image (FEN misperception)
failure_modes.description: A2/B2/C2.4 — The agent reconstructed the position by reading the PNG and produced a FEN that does not contain the forced mate the task guarantees. Because the analyzed board state was wrong, Stockfish never surfaced Rd5#, and every downstream move evaluation (Nf5/d4f5, Qd1, Qxc3) was reasoning over the wrong position. The agent repeatedly suspected specific piece misreads but could not resolve them.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: considering if the pieces on b3, a3 and h5 could be different, like a black knight instead of queen, but the piece has a crown

counterfactual.X: Correct the single misidentified piece so the FEN matches the true position
counterfactual.Y: Stockfish reports the forced mate, the agent identifies Rd5 and writes it to /app/answer.txt, passing the verifier

failure_modes.name: Misread board into FEN
failure_modes.description: The agent decoded the diagram with h1 as top-left and built FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K b - - 0 1. Stockfish on that position never shows a forced win (depth 35 bestmove d4f5, score cp 8), so the agent never converged on the required Rd5.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Top-left is h1. Bottom-right is a8.

failure_modes.name: Wrong FEN from image
failure_modes.description: The agent manually decoded the PNG into an incorrect position (e.g., Black queen on b3) and fed that FEN to Stockfish, which evaluated the position as roughly equal rather than a forced black win.

failure_modes.name: Vision/transcription error built a FEN with no winning move for Black
failure_modes.description: The agent transcribed the PNG into FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K which Stockfish evaluates as ~+0.08 for White — a position with no forced win for Black, contradicting the task's guarantee. This wrong board became the substrate for every downstream search, and the agent oscillated between trusting Stockfish, suspecting individual squares (was h5 really a queen? is b3 really a queen?), and even trying a 'rotated' FEN — never identifying the actual misread.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The initial +0.13 score suggests a slight white advantage or a draw, which directly contradicts the winning requirement for black.

failure_modes.name: Five-minute Stockfish hang
failure_modes.description: A multipv Stockfish subprocess at step 12 was auto-cancelled after 5.0 minutes without finishing, consuming roughly half the 600s agent budget on non-delivering analysis.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

failure_modes.name: Never wrote answer file
failure_modes.description: The task explicitly requires writing the final algebraic move to /app/answer.txt, but the trajectory contains no write to that path and ends on a text-only agent step when the harness raises AgentTimeoutError.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: When you have determined the final answer, write it to `/app/answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: After Stockfish PV showed d8d5, run `echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt`
counterfactual.Y: Continued open-ended board re-analysis and Stockfish runs without writing any answer file

failure_modes.name: Misinterpreted validation signal from engine output
failure_modes.description: The agent ran meaningful validation with Stockfish but treated the engine's low evaluation and alternate best move as evidence that the position/FEN was wrong, instead of converging on the puzzle answer. The correct UCI move d8d5 appeared in its own Stockfish output, but it never converted that to algebraic Rd5 or wrote the answer file.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: info depth 9 seldepth 11 multipv 1 score cp 44 nodes 5001 nps 555666 hashfull 1 tbhits 0 time 9 pv d8d5 h5h4 b3b6 h4g3 b6d6 g3d6 d5d6

counterfactual.X: At the point d8d5 appeared in the engine line, convert it to algebraic notation as Rd5 and write exactly that to /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier's exact normalized comparison against expected_answer.txt would have passed.

failure_modes.name: Misread validation signal from engine analysis
failure_modes.description: The agent treated Stockfish's near-equal evaluation on its constructed FEN as evidence that the task/FEN must be wrong, even though the expected move appeared in Stockfish principal variations as d8d5, and continued searching rather than converting the candidate to algebraic notation Rd5.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Stockfish's evaluation of +0.08 seems to contradict the requirement for a guaranteed win for Black.

counterfactual.X: At the engine-output step, recognize d8d5 as rook move Rd5 and write echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The exact-string verifier would have matched the expected answer Rd5.

failure_modes.name: Five-minute Stockfish hang
failure_modes.description: At step 12 the agent launched `go depth 12 multipv 15`; the shell command was auto-cancelled after 5 minutes without finishing, consuming most remaining budget before the 600s agent timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

failure_modes.name: Hung subprocess command drained the entire time budget
failure_modes.description: At step 12 the agent ran a Python/Stockfish 'go depth 12 multipv 15' script whose stdout-reading and terminate logic deadlocked; the shell command hung until cancelled after 5 minutes, consuming essentially all remaining agent time and directly causing the 600s AgentTimeoutError
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

counterfactual.X: Use a bounded invocation (e.g. stockfish 'go movetime 2000' or a subprocess call with a hard kill/timeout and proper pipe draining) instead of an unbounded multipv read loop that deadlocks
counterfactual.Y: The command returns in seconds, preserving ~5 minutes of budget for the agent to commit an answer and write /app/answer.txt

failure_modes.name: No answer delivered
failure_modes.description: Despite the instruction to write the final move to /app/answer.txt, the agent spent the full 600s budget re-running analysis and orientation checks, then hit AgentTimeoutError without ever submitting an answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I'm going to run Stockfish again, but this time with a white turn to see if the evaluation changes significantly.

counterfactual.X: Write the identified winning move (Rd5) to /app/answer.txt, e.g. echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt
counterfactual.Y: Continued open-ended re-analysis and Stockfish runs until the harness timed out at 600 seconds without writing /app/answer.txt

failure_modes.name: Never wrote answer file
failure_modes.description: Despite instruction.md requiring a single move in /app/answer.txt, the agent spent the full 600s budget on engine installs and analysis loops, then hit AgentTimeoutError on step 18 without ever echoing a final move. The verifier’s first gate is file existence (test.sh:11-14).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: When you have determined the final answer, write it to `/app/answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: Run `echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt` once the winning rook move is identified (Stockfish shallow PV d8d5 matches expected Rd5).
counterfactual.Y: Continued Stockfish re-analysis and timed out without creating /app/answer.txt.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent attempted legitimate Stockfish analysis on its constructed FEN; no attempt to bypass the verifier or read /tests/expected_answer.txt.

notes_for_aggregation: Vision/perception failure on a chess-image GAIA task: VLM misidentified piece placement → wrong FEN → solver-tool consensus that contradicted the prompt's guaranteed-win premise → agent looped on re-analysis and timed out without writing any answer.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
